channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistani Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Iran-US Mediation Efforts
item.description: Pakistan's interior minister traveled to Tehran amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to mediate between Iran and the United States as tensions continue to escalate in the Gulf region.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Vows to Accelerate Military Buildup Citing Russian Threat
item.description: Germany pledged to speed up its military expansion efforts, citing ongoing security concerns related to Russia as the driving factor behind the accelerated defense spending.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Condemns Israeli Aggression Against Lebanon
item.description: Saudi Arabia issued a strong condemnation of what it called Israeli aggression against Lebanon, adding regional pressure amid ongoing cross-border hostilities.

item.title: 🇦🇱 Albanians Protest Jared Kushner-Backed Luxury Resort on Nature Reserve
item.description: Thousands of Albanians protested plans for a luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner on the country's coast, raising concerns about environmental impact on protected areas.

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore Orders Social Media Platforms to Block Foreign Posts Targeting Indian Community
item.description: Singapore's government directed social media platforms to block foreign-originated content targeting the Indian community, invoking new online harm prevention laws.

item.title: ⚔️ US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down four Iranian attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz, marking another flare-up in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Accuses China of Coordinated Operation Near Strategic South China Sea Islands
item.description: A Chinese coast guard ship and survey vessel carried out what Taiwan called the first coordinated provocation near strategically located islands in the South China Sea.

item.title: 🏛️ White House AI Policy Adviser Krishnan to Leave Position
item.description: White House AI policy adviser Sriram Krishnan plans to leave his position at the end of June, the Information reported on Saturday.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Taiwan Needs to Spend Smarter on Defense, US Diplomat Says
item.description: Taiwan must "spend smarter" on defense and learn lessons from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts about drone warfare to maintain the military balance with China, the de facto US ambassador to Taipei said.

item.title: 🛢️ Rosneft Chief: US Energy Companies Benefit Most From Hormuz Closure
item.description: Russian oil giant Rosneft's CEO Igor Sechin said US energy companies were the main beneficiaries of the Strait of Hormuz closure but warned continued tensions would undermine long-term oil demand.

item.title: ☢️ Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Restores Power Supply After Outage
item.description: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its Ferrosplavnaya-1 power line on Saturday, securing electricity supply to the facility amid ongoing conflict in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Five Civilians Killed in Russian Attacks on Kherson Region
item.description: Russian forces killed five people in three separate attacks on Ukraine's southern Kherson region on Friday, according to the regional governor, as fighting continues in the occupied territories.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korean Protesters Demand Election Re-Run After Ballot Shortage
item.description: Protesters in South Korea rallied for a second day outside a ballot-counting site, demanding a re-run of local elections held earlier this week after reports of ballot shortages disrupted voting.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Israeli Strike Kills Five at Wedding in Gaza
item.description: At least five people were killed and others wounded when Israeli forces bombed a wedding tent in Gaza City. The attack comes amid ongoing conflict in the region.

item.title: 🇯🇲 Jamaica Experiences Island-Wide Power Blackout
item.description: Jamaica is experiencing a nationwide power outage, with the energy minister confirming the blackout on social media. No cause has been given yet.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Urges Firing of Intelligence Community Employees
item.description: President Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants the acting director of national intelligence to begin firing large numbers of employees as part of a shake-up of the US intelligence community.

item.title: 🤖 Meta Considers Major Stock Offering for AI Infrastructure
item.description: Meta is weighing raising tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to fund AI infrastructure ambitions, according to Financial Times. The move comes as tech giants race to expand AI capabilities.

item.title: 🇱🇧✈️ Lebanon Opens Second International Airport Amid Israel-Hezbollah War
item.description: Lebanon inaugurated Qlayaat airport near the Syrian border as a second international hub, converting it from military airbase to passenger use as the country seeks to shift traffic from Beirut amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇳🎓 India Youth March Against Exam Leaks in Delhi Cockroaches Protest
item.description: Thousands of young Indians protested in New Delhi under the angry cockroaches movement, demanding accountability for exam paper leaks that have undermined educational opportunities and government job prospects.

item.title: 🛢️💰 US Companies Benefit From Strait of Hormuz Disruptions, Rosneft Chief Says
item.description: Igor Sechin claimed American energy companies are profiting from disruptions to the key shipping route, while China is better prepared than other nations for potential supply interruptions.

item.title: 🇵🇸🏛️ Hamas Says Cairo Talks Begin on Gaza Ceasefire Implementation
item.description: Hamas announced that talks in Cairo are focusing on ending Israeli violations, opening crossings, delivering aid, and completing the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.

item.title: 🇵🇸🇮🇱 Israeli forces kill Palestinian infant in West Bank shooting
item.description: A seven-month-old Palestinian baby was killed and his parents wounded when Israeli soldiers opened fire on their vehicle in Hebron. The Israeli military said the incident is under review and those injured were uninvolved civilians.

item.title: 🇲🇲💥 Explosion at Myanmar building storing mining explosives kills over 45
item.description: A blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar near the Chinese border killed more than 45 people and injured 70 others. The Taang National Liberation Army said gelignite stored for mining operations detonated.

item.title: 🇳🇪💀 At least 49 migrants die of thirst in Niger desert after truck breakdown
item.description: Forty-nine people died of thirst in the Sahara desert after their truck broke down while traveling from Mali to Niger for Eid al-Adha celebrations. Two survivors walked more than 50 km to alert authorities.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳🌊 Taiwan accuses China of coordinated operation near South China Sea outpost
item.description: Taiwan's coast guard accused China of attempting to create a 'false illusion' of jurisdiction over waters surrounding a strategic South China Sea outpost, as a Chinese coast guard ship and survey vessel carried out coordinated operations.
